anomaly_signals: smb_sweep: 5 port 445 connections in 12 seconds from single host — subnet scanning pattern
novel_behavior: ZERO baseline entries of AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01 initiating outbound SMB connections
cross_subnet_smb: connection from 10.0.4.30 to 10.0.8.10 (admin subnet) violates network segmentation baseline
sequential_ips: .52, .53, .61, .78, .10 — sequential scan pattern, not organic traffic
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: SMB subnet sweep from AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01. This host has NEVER initiated outbound SMB in the 30-day baseline. The sequential IP pattern (.52, .53, .61, .78) is a textbook SMB sweep — attacker is enumerating which systems have open file shares. Critically, .10 is in the admin subnet (10.0.8.10 = AIIMS-DC-01), indicating attacker already knows or has guessed the domain controller address. The 3-second inter-connection interval suggests automated tooling, not human clicking.

anomaly_signals: service_account_to_dc: svc_backup$ has NEVER authenticated to AIIMS-DC-01 in baseline
source_anomaly: source is AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01 (clinical server) — not the backup server
lateral_movement_chain: this is the second hop — attacker moved from 10.0.4.47 → PATIENT-MGMT-01 → DC-01
ntlm_to_dc: NTLM auth to domain controller (vs Kerberos) indicates pass-the-hash attack possible
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: Attacker authenticates svc_backup$ credentials to the Domain Controller from AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01. This is the second lateral movement hop. NTLM authentication to a domain controller (rather than Kerberos) is a strong indicator of pass-the-hash — the attacker likely extracted the NTLM hash from AIIMS-PATIENT-MGMT-01's memory after the Day 1 compromise and is now using it. Domain Controllers should never receive NTLM auth from clinical servers for service accounts.

anomaly_signals: domain_enum_on_dc: net user /domain executed ON the domain controller — maximum visibility into all accounts
service_acct_interactive: service account running interactive net commands on DC is extremely anomalous
post_lateral_enum: executed 2 minutes after successful SMB auth to DC — classic post-access enumeration
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: 'net user /domain' executed on AIIMS-DC-01 by svc_backup$ dumps all domain user accounts. On a domain controller, this returns every account in AD — giving the attacker a full user list to target for credential attacks, select high-value accounts to impersonate, or identify service accounts for further compromise. No backup service ever needs to enumerate domain users.

output_captured: User: AIIMS\svc_backup$  Groups: Domain Users, Backup Operators, Domain Admins (via nested group)
anomaly_signals: privilege_discovery: /all flag reveals group memberships — attacker confirming they have Domain Admin via nested groups
nested_group_da: svc_backup$ is in Backup Operators which has Domain Admin nested — critical misconfiguration exploitation
dc_execution: whoami /all on domain controller confirms full domain compromise
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: 'whoami /all' confirms the attacker has discovered they have Domain Admin rights via a nested group (Backup Operators → Domain Admins). This is a critical configuration weakness in AIIMS's Active Directory — the backup service account was given Domain Admin rights for convenience. The attacker now knows they have keys to the entire kingdom. This output would have been logged and a DLP-aware SIEM should have flagged 'Domain Admins' appearing in process output from a service account.

task_description: Keeps your Microsoft Edge browser up to date.
anomaly_signals: masquerade_name: task name 'MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA' mimics legitimate Edge updater — lolbas masquerading
cmd_powershell_chain: task runs cmd.exe → powershell with base64 encoded payload
encoded_payload: base64 decodes to IEX WebClient downloading from 185.220.101.47 (Tor exit node — seen in Day 12)
system_persistence: task runs as SYSTEM at every logon — maximum privilege persistence
created_by_service_acct: scheduled tasks should be created by SYSTEM or admin_raj, not svc_backup$
dc_persistence: persistence established on domain controller = entire domain persistence
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: This is the attacker establishing persistence on the Domain Controller itself. The task name is a deliberate masquerade of the legitimate 'MicrosoftEdgeUpdateTaskMachineUA' scheduled task. The base64-encoded PowerShell downloads a payload from 185.220.101.47 — the same Tor exit node used for C2 in Day 12. This backward chaining is forensically significant: the C2 infrastructure was already set up before the first lateral movement. Task created by svc_backup$ (a service account) creating a SYSTEM-level scheduled task is a near-certain malicious indicator. Containment opportunity: killing this task on Day 3 would have stopped persistence and all subsequent compromise.
